heading: Scholarships authorized
label: 1070d–33 Scholarships authorized
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1070d–33, , as added , , ; amended , , , authorized the award of scholarships under Robert C. Byrd Honors Scholarship Program, prior to the general revision of this part by .Pub. L. 89–329, title IV, § 419CPub. L. 98–558, title VIII, § 801(a)98 Stat. 2900Pub. L. 99–145, title XVI, § 1627(b)99 Stat. 779Pub. L. 99–498Oct. 30, 1984Nov. 8, 1985

Amendments2009—Subsec. (b)(1). inserted “and” after semicolon at end.Pub. L. 111–391993—Subsec. (b). substituted “for a period of not less than 1 or more than 4 years during the first 4 years of study” for “for a period of not more than 4 years for the first 4 years of study” and inserted at end “The State educational agency administering the program in a State shall have discretion to determine the period of the award (within the limits specified in the preceding sentence), except that—Pub. L. 103–208“(1) if the amount appropriated for this subpart for any fiscal year exceeds the amount appropriated for this subpart for fiscal year 1993, the Secretary shall identify to each State educational agency the number of scholarships available to that State under that are attributable to such excess;section 1070d–34(b) of this title“(2) the State educational agency shall award not less than that number of scholarships for a period of 4 years.”1992—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Scholarships under this section shall be awarded for a period of one academic year for the first year of study at an institution of higher education.”Pub. L. 102–325

children.heading: Program authority
children.label: a Program authority
children.text: The Secretary is authorized, in accordance with the provisions of this subpart, to make grants to States to enable the States to award scholarships to individuals who have demonstrated outstanding academic achievement and who show promise of continued academic achievement.

if the amount appropriated for this subpart for any fiscal year exceeds the amount appropriated for this subpart for fiscal year 1993, the Secretary shall identify to each State educational agency the number of scholarships available to that State under that are attributable to such excess; andsection 1070d–34(b) of this title
the State educational agency shall award not less than that number of scholarships for a period of 4 years.

children.heading: Use at any institution permitted
children.label: c Use at any institution permitted
children.text: A student awarded a scholarship under this subpart may attend any institution of higher education.

children.heading: Byrd Scholars
children.label: d Byrd Scholars
children.text: Individuals awarded scholarships under this subpart shall be known as “Byrd Scholars”.
